hidden_constraints.oracle_response: Spaces are replaced with '+', and '@' symbols are replaced with '%40'.

context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the sublitition rule in the path_param feature block
context_pointers.actual_meaning: This refers to the feature C004 which explicitly handles the logic for substituting {{userid}} into the path string.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature10_status_verification.json.description: Response status assertions succeed for matching values and report a normalized assertion failure for mismatches.

cases.expected_output:
error=assertion_failed
message=Expected response status code to match 'greater than 300', but was 200

feature11_response_header_verification.j.description: Response headers and content types can be checked exactly or by containment, with normalized failures for absent or mismatched values.

feature12_response_body_verification.jso.description: Whole response bodies can be checked exactly or by substring containment, with normalized failures on mismatches.

cases.expected_output:
method=GET
request_path=/plaintext-response-body
status=200
body=Here's a plaintext response body.
verification=body_matched

input.expected: This is a different plaintext response body.

cases.expected_output:
error=assertion_failed
message=Actual response body did not match expected response body.\nExpected: This is a different plaintext response body.\nActual: Here's a plaintext response body.

feature13_json_body_verification.json.description: JSON response elements selected by path expressions can be checked as scalar values or collections.

cases.expected_output:
error=assertion_failed
message=Expected elements selected by '$.Places[0:].Name' to match 'a collection containing "Atlantis"', but was [Sun City, Pleasure Meadow]

feature14_xml_body_verification.json.description: XML response elements selected by path expressions can be checked as scalar values or collections.

cases.expected_output:
error=assertion_failed
message=Expected elements selected by '//Place/Name' to match 'a collection containing "Atlantis"', but was [Sun City, Pleasure Meadow]

feature15_json_extraction.json.description: JSON response values, response headers, and full response metadata can be extracted after a successful request.

cases.expected_output:
method=GET
request_path=/json-response-body
status=200
selector=$.Places[0].Name
value=Sun City

feature16_xml_extraction.json.description: XML response values can be extracted with path expressions, including single values and multiple matches.

cases.expected_output:
method=GET
request_path=/xml-response-body
status=200
selector=//Place[1]/Name
value=Sun City

error=extraction_failed
message=XPath expression '//Place/DoesNotExist' did not yield any results.

feature17_deserialization.json.description: JSON and XML responses can be converted into caller-defined data objects when the response content type is recognized.

cases.expected_output:
method=GET
request_path=/json-deserialization
content_type=application/json
country=United States
places_count=2

error=deserialization_failed
message=Unable to deserialize response with Content-Type 'application/something'

feature18_json_schema.json.description: JSON responses can be validated against a supplied schema and produce normalized verification errors for schema, data, or content-type problems.

cases.expected_output:
error=response_verification_failed
message=Expected response Content-Type header to contain 'json', but was 'application/something'

feature1_http_methods.json.description: HTTP clients can issue the requested verb to a target endpoint and expose the response status.

feature2_request_headers.json.description: Request builders can attach ordinary headers, content metadata, and accept preferences that are seen by the server.

feature3_authorization_headers.json.description: Request builders can derive authorization header values from credentials or bearer tokens.

cases.expected_output:
method=GET
request_path=/basic-auth
authorization=basic
value=Basic dXNlcm5hbWU6cGFzc3dvcmQ=
status=200

feature4_cookies.json.description: A request can carry cookies supplied in supported input shapes and the server receives the same name-value pair.

feature5_query_parameters.json.description: Query parameters are appended to outgoing URLs, and repeated parameter names use the last supplied value.

feature6_path_parameters.json.description: Named placeholders in a target path are replaced with supplied values before the HTTP request is sent.

feature7_request_bodies.json.description: POST requests can send plain text and JSON bodies that are observable by the server.

cases.expected_output:
method=POST
request_path=/plaintext-request-body
body=Here's a plaintext request body.
status=201

feature8_form_data.json.description: Key-value fields can be sent as URL-encoded form data with the expected request content type.

feature9_user_agent.json.description: A request can set a product-style user agent directly or through reusable request defaults.

_constraints.input_condition: All successful feature requests (http_method, request_header, auth_header, cookie, query_param, path_param, request_body, form_data, user_agent)
_constraints.output_rule: Output must be multi-line with explicit trailing newline, containing specific lines in strict order: method, request_path, [feature-specific output], status.
The 'method' line in output must exactly match the value of the 'METHOD' key in the input
The 'request_path' line must exactly match the 'path' key in the input (for simple paths) or 'expected_path' (for path_param)

_constraints.input_condition: feature input has 'path_param' and 'parameters' array with 'userid'='1'
_constraints.output_rule: The output request_path must be exactly '/user/1', substituting {{userid}} with the parameter value

Output query string line must follow format 'query=name=value'. If name appears multiple times, use the last value. Do not encode '=' in the static name or value unless part of the value string itself.
Output 'body' must be URL-encoded string of fields. Spaces become '+'. '@' becomes '%40'. Key=Value pairs separated by '&'.

_constraints.input_condition: feature input has 'auth_header' with 'scheme'='basic' and user/pass
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'value' must be Base64 encoded string of 'username:password', prefixed with 'Basic '; scheme line must be ' authorization=basic'
Output 'user_agent' line must be 'product/version' joined by '/'
The 'status' line must output the 'response_status' integer value from the input

_constraints.input_condition: feature input has 'status_verification' where 'check'='integer' and actual matches expected
_constraints.output_rule: Output must include 'verification=status_matched' after the status line

_constraints.input_condition: feature input has 'status_verification' where check logic fails
_constraints.output_rule: Output must start with 'error=assertion_failed' followed by 'message=...', no status line or verification line
Output must include 'header=name' line where name matches input header name
Output must include 'value=value' line containing the input value

_constraints.input_condition: feature input has 'response_header_verification' where header missing or mismatch
_constraints.output_rule: Output must start with 'error=assertion_failed' followed by 'message=' explaining the mismatch or absence
Output must include 'body=' line containing the raw input body string

_constraints.input_condition: feature input has 'json_body_verification' where collection check fails (e.g., 'item_equal' vs missing element)
_constraints.output_rule: Output must start with 'error=assertion_failed', message must indicate expected collection vs actual collection, formatted with newlines between Expected and Actual

_constraints.input_condition: feature input has 'json_extraction' or 'xml_extraction' and succeeds
_constraints.output_rule: Output must include 'selector=path' line immediately after status line, then 'value=value'

_constraints.input_condition: feature input has 'json_extraction'/'xml_extraction' yielding no results
_constraints.output_rule: Output must start with 'error=extraction_failed', message must contain the XPath expression in quotes stating it yielded no results
Output must include 'custom_header=name' and 'custom_header=value' lines
Output must include 'content_type=application/json'

_constraints.input_condition: feature input has 'deserialization' with unknown or unsupported format
_constraints.output_rule: Output must start with 'error=deserialization_failed', message must state inability to deserialize for the Content-Type

_constraints.input_condition: feature input has 'deserialization' with format 'json' and valid data
_constraints.output_rule: Output must include flattened key-value pairs like 'country=...' and 'places_count=...'
Output must start with 'error=response_verification_failed', message must mention Expected Content-Type containing 'json' but was 'application/something'
Output must include 'verification=json_schema_matched'
Output must include 'content_type=application/x-www-form-urlencoded'

hide.replacement: a hardcoded path pattern for lookup tests
standard URL encoding behavior for form bodies
